text: Effective risk management is a crucial skill in both business and daily life. It begins with identifying potential risks that could harm a project or goal. Once risks are identified, you can develop mitigation strategies to reduce their impact or likelihood. For example, a company might diversify its suppliers to avoid depending on one source. However, not every risk can be fully avoided, so it's wise to create contingency plans. These are backup actions you will take if a risk actually occurs. Finally, continuous monitoring is essential. Risks can change over time, so regularly checking your environment allows you to update your plans before problems become serious.
